data_json.content:
### Overview
Resume the agent-harness implementation track from a planning-complete state: the epic is refined, the architecture decisions are documented, the CH1-CH12 dependency graph is established, and the next session should execute the roadmap in order while preserving the single-session-model goal for both interactive and autonomous modes.

### Findings
1. Epic planning and decomposition are complete, with sequencing and child dependencies documented in [epic description](.ticket/tickets/0f4b3c5b-c5e9-45c4-968c-a8878f359de8/description.md).
2. The owning spec exists and captures contract intent plus assumption/validation sections: [spec body](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/body.md), [resolved decisions](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/sections/resolved-decisions-and-assumptions.md), [validation design](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/sections/validation-design.md), [interview assumptions](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/sections/refinement-interview.md).
3. Immediate actionable frontier is CH1; downstream tickets are intentionally blocked by dependency edges.
4. Board is currently clear for this track; no ownership conflicts are required to resolve before check-in.
5. Persisted session-store data is low-signal for this track; tickets/specs are the canonical restart source.

### Decisions To Preserve
1. Rust must own session lifecycle and loop execution; external Copilot session lifecycle APIs are not available. Source: [research ticket](.ticket/tickets/cba080b5-3c38-495d-8b67-d690b52de4d6/ticket.toml).
2. D1 crate layout under agent-harness, D2 NDJSON plus checkpoints, D3 sandbox boundary, D4 concurrency and budget defaults, D5 guidance precedence are the active assumptions of record. Source: [resolved decisions](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/sections/resolved-decisions-and-assumptions.md).
3. Validation must include browser evidence gates at the correct stage (CH11), not deferred beyond release readiness.

### Blockers And Risks
1. High risk: top aligned spec sections still need concrete contract/validation/ticket linkage to reduce ambiguity during implementation: [spec body](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/body.md#L11).
2. Medium risk: assumption-driven decisions need stakeholder confirmation if policy defaults differ (sandbox/network, budget defaults, retention policy).
3. Medium risk: skipping required evidence capture during CH11 will invalidate release readiness.
4. Operational note: unrelated ticket state-transition failures in other tracks should not block this epic unless they affect shared files.

### Suggested Long-Horizon Goal
Deliver the epic [0f4b3c5b](.ticket/tickets/0f4b3c5b-c5e9-45c4-968c-a8878f359de8/ticket.toml): one minimal operator interface that supports both on-demand conversation and long-running autonomous loops from the same session model, with auditable safety, budget, and recovery controls.

### Key Entities
1. [Epic manifest](.ticket/tickets/0f4b3c5b-c5e9-45c4-968c-a8878f359de8/ticket.toml): source of truth for epic state, priority, dependencies.
2. [Epic narrative](.ticket/tickets/0f4b3c5b-c5e9-45c4-968c-a8878f359de8/description.md): roadmap, sequencing, and CH ticket map.
3. [Owning spec body](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/body.md): contract anchor requiring final top-level linkage hardening.
4. [Research prerequisite](.ticket/tickets/cba080b5-3c38-495d-8b67-d690b52de4d6/ticket.toml): constraints on control-surface architecture.
5. [Design blueprint](DESIGN_AGENT_HARNESS.md): architecture inspiration and boundaries.
6. [CH1 ticket](.ticket/tickets/a5f08931-24af-4b96-a156-9107c776f946/ticket.toml): first executable implementation slice.
7. [CH2 ticket](.ticket/tickets/c684b092-7f5a-4ebe-aa6d-494f666f5dc8/ticket.toml): immediate follow-on once CH1 passes.
8. [Prompt workflow guide](.agents/prompts/ticket-next.prompt.md): actionable-ticket-first execution convention.
9. [Continuation guide](.agents/prompts/next.prompt.md): iterative continuation convention.
10. [Session optimization guidance](.agents/instructions/session-optimization.instructions.md): avoid replaying noisy transcript material.

### Full Roadmap (Execution Order)
1. Check in on CH1 and claim only intended files for the foundation slice.
2. Patch owning spec aligned top sections with explicit provided contracts, required validation, and linked CH tickets.
3. Implement CH1 workspace and shared contract scaffolding.
4. Run CH1 fast checks.
5. Run CH1 primary tests and record evidence.
6. Advance CH1 state per workflow and verify epic graph health.
7. Implement CH2 unified session/mode core.
8. Execute CH3 and CH4 in dependency order after CH2.
9. Execute CH5 after CH4.
10. Execute CH6 after CH1 plus CH2.
11. Execute CH7 and CH8 after CH6.
12. Execute CH9 after CH7 plus CH8.
13. Execute CH10 after CH6.
14. Execute CH11 release validation after CH7 plus CH8 plus CH9 plus CH10.
15. Execute CH12 documentation and rollout checklist after CH11.
16. Move epic forward only when all child evidence is complete and linked.

### Completion Status
1. CH1 check-in and file ownership: not-started; board is clear.
2. Spec top-section hardening: not-started; identified as open quality gap.
3. CH1 implementation and validation: not-started.
4. CH2 core implementation: not-started.
5. CH3 provider/guidance/budget hooks: not-started.
6. CH4 MCP routing envelope: not-started.
7. CH5 sandbox enforcement: not-started.
8. CH6 server lifecycle/fanout: not-started.
9. CH7 TUI surface: not-started.
10. CH8 WASM parity: not-started.
11. CH9 diff parity: not-started.
12. CH10 reliability/recovery: not-started.
13. CH11 end-to-end plus browser evidence: not-started.
14. CH12 docs/runbooks/rollout: not-started.
15. Epic closure readiness: not-started.

### Definition Of Done
1. CH1 through CH12 completed in dependency order with required state progression and attached validation evidence.
2. Owning spec has concrete aligned top sections and full traceability to implementation tickets and checks.
3. Unified session-model behavior verified across interactive and autonomous modes.
4. Policy, budget, sandbox, reconnect, and recovery guarantees validated with evidence.
5. Browser and Playwright release evidence captured for transient and steady-state UI behavior.
6. Epic is implementation-complete and ready for review/closure with no unresolved blockers, or explicitly marked blocked with evidence.

### Board And Persisted Session Notes
1. Board state is clear for immediate check-in on this track.
2. Persisted session history should be treated as diagnostic only; rely on ticket/spec artifacts first.
3. If large command outputs recur, keep artifact pointers and extract only durable findings into tickets/specs.

Execute the next actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.

data.content:
### Overview
Resume the agent-harness implementation track from a planning-complete state: the epic is refined, the architecture decisions are documented, the CH1-CH12 dependency graph is established, and the next session should execute the roadmap in order while preserving the single-session-model goal for both interactive and autonomous modes.

### Findings
1. Epic planning and decomposition are complete, with sequencing and child dependencies documented in [epic description](.ticket/tickets/0f4b3c5b-c5e9-45c4-968c-a8878f359de8/description.md).
2. The owning spec exists and captures contract intent plus assumption/validation sections: [spec body](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/body.md), [resolved decisions](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/sections/resolved-decisions-and-assumptions.md), [validation design](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/sections/validation-design.md), [interview assumptions](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/sections/refinement-interview.md).
3. Immediate actionable frontier is CH1; downstream tickets are intentionally blocked by dependency edges.
4. Board is currently clear for this track; no ownership conflicts are required to resolve before check-in.
5. Persisted session-store data is low-signal for this track; tickets/specs are the canonical restart source.

### Decisions To Preserve
1. Rust must own session lifecycle and loop execution; external Copilot session lifecycle APIs are not available. Source: [research ticket](.ticket/tickets/cba080b5-3c38-495d-8b67-d690b52de4d6/ticket.toml).
2. D1 crate layout under agent-harness, D2 NDJSON plus checkpoints, D3 sandbox boundary, D4 concurrency and budget defaults, D5 guidance precedence are the active assumptions of record. Source: [resolved decisions](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/sections/resolved-decisions-and-assumptions.md).
3. Validation must include browser evidence gates at the correct stage (CH11), not deferred beyond release readiness.

### Blockers And Risks
1. High risk: top aligned spec sections still need concrete contract/validation/ticket linkage to reduce ambiguity during implementation: [spec body](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/body.md#L11).
2. Medium risk: assumption-driven decisions need stakeholder confirmation if policy defaults differ (sandbox/network, budget defaults, retention policy).
3. Medium risk: skipping required evidence capture during CH11 will invalidate release readiness.
4. Operational note: unrelated ticket state-transition failures in other tracks should not block this epic unless they affect shared files.

### Suggested Long-Horizon Goal
Deliver the epic [0f4b3c5b](.ticket/tickets/0f4b3c5b-c5e9-45c4-968c-a8878f359de8/ticket.toml): one minimal operator interface that supports both on-demand conversation and long-running autonomous loops from the same session model, with auditable safety, budget, and recovery controls.

### Key Entities
1. [Epic manifest](.ticket/tickets/0f4b3c5b-c5e9-45c4-968c-a8878f359de8/ticket.toml): source of truth for epic state, priority, dependencies.
2. [Epic narrative](.ticket/tickets/0f4b3c5b-c5e9-45c4-968c-a8878f359de8/description.md): roadmap, sequencing, and CH ticket map.
3. [Owning spec body](.spec/specs/6f286eee-115b-4476-a6fc-9bd6796b5f3f/body.md): contract anchor requiring final top-level linkage hardening.
4. [Research prerequisite](.ticket/tickets/cba080b5-3c38-495d-8b67-d690b52de4d6/ticket.toml): constraints on control-surface architecture.
5. [Design blueprint](DESIGN_AGENT_HARNESS.md): architecture inspiration and boundaries.
6. [CH1 ticket](.ticket/tickets/a5f08931-24af-4b96-a156-9107c776f946/ticket.toml): first executable implementation slice.
7. [CH2 ticket](.ticket/tickets/c684b092-7f5a-4ebe-aa6d-494f666f5dc8/ticket.toml): immediate follow-on once CH1 passes.
8. [Prompt workflow guide](.agents/prompts/ticket-next.prompt.md): actionable-ticket-first execution convention.
9. [Continuation guide](.agents/prompts/next.prompt.md): iterative continuation convention.
10. [Session optimization guidance](.agents/instructions/session-optimization.instructions.md): avoid replaying noisy transcript material.

### Full Roadmap (Execution Order)
1. Check in on CH1 and claim only intended files for the foundation slice.
2. Patch owning spec aligned top sections with explicit provided contracts, required validation, and linked CH tickets.
3. Implement CH1 workspace and shared contract scaffolding.
4. Run CH1 fast checks.
5. Run CH1 primary tests and record evidence.
6. Advance CH1 state per workflow and verify epic graph health.
7. Implement CH2 unified session/mode core.
8. Execute CH3 and CH4 in dependency order after CH2.
9. Execute CH5 after CH4.
10. Execute CH6 after CH1 plus CH2.
11. Execute CH7 and CH8 after CH6.
12. Execute CH9 after CH7 plus CH8.
13. Execute CH10 after CH6.
14. Execute CH11 release validation after CH7 plus CH8 plus CH9 plus CH10.
15. Execute CH12 documentation and rollout checklist after CH11.
16. Move epic forward only when all child evidence is complete and linked.

### Completion Status
1. CH1 check-in and file ownership: not-started; board is clear.
2. Spec top-section hardening: not-started; identified as open quality gap.
3. CH1 implementation and validation: not-started.
4. CH2 core implementation: not-started.
5. CH3 provider/guidance/budget hooks: not-started.
6. CH4 MCP routing envelope: not-started.
7. CH5 sandbox enforcement: not-started.
8. CH6 server lifecycle/fanout: not-started.
9. CH7 TUI surface: not-started.
10. CH8 WASM parity: not-started.
11. CH9 diff parity: not-started.
12. CH10 reliability/recovery: not-started.
13. CH11 end-to-end plus browser evidence: not-started.
14. CH12 docs/runbooks/rollout: not-started.
15. Epic closure readiness: not-started.

### Definition Of Done
1. CH1 through CH12 completed in dependency order with required state progression and attached validation evidence.
2. Owning spec has concrete aligned top sections and full traceability to implementation tickets and checks.
3. Unified session-model behavior verified across interactive and autonomous modes.
4. Policy, budget, sandbox, reconnect, and recovery guarantees validated with evidence.
5. Browser and Playwright release evidence captured for transient and steady-state UI behavior.
6. Epic is implementation-complete and ready for review/closure with no unresolved blockers, or explicitly marked blocked with evidence.

### Board And Persisted Session Notes
1. Board state is clear for immediate check-in on this track.
2. Persisted session history should be treated as diagnostic only; rely on ticket/spec artifacts first.
3. If large command outputs recur, keep artifact pointers and extract only durable findings into tickets/specs.

Execute the next actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.
